# @zakkster/lite-headless / alert-dialog

An interruptive confirm / destroy dialog. This is `createDialog` with the
alert-dialog contract locked in: `role="alertdialog"`, always modal, and a
backdrop click does **not** dismiss (the user must choose an action).

Same surface and same painted contract as dialog -- an alert dialog *is* a
dialog with a stricter dismiss policy and a more assertive role.

## Quick example

```js
import { createAlertDialog } from "@zakkster/lite-headless/alert-dialog";

const d = createAlertDialog({
    // closeOnOutsideClick defaults to false; opt back in with `true`
    closeOnEscape: true,   // Escape acts as cancel (default)
    onOpenChange: (open) => console.log(open),
});

d.attachTrigger(deleteBtn);
d.attachContent(panel);     // role="alertdialog", aria-modal
d.attachTitle(h2);          // wires aria-labelledby
d.attachDescription(p);     // wires aria-describedby
d.attachClose(cancelBtn);

d.setOpen(true);
```

## Options

Identical to dialog, with these forced/changed defaults:

| Option | Value | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `role` | `"alertdialog"` | forced |
| `modal` | `true` | forced (an alert must trap) |
| `closeOnOutsideClick` | `false` | default; pass `true` to allow backdrop dismiss |
| `closeOnEscape` | `true` | inherited; pass `false` for a hard confirm |

All other dialog options (`placement`, `container`, `initialFocus`,
`finalFocus`, `transition`, `open`, `defaultOpen`) pass straight through.

## API

Same as `DialogInstance`:

```ts
{
    open(): boolean
    status(): "closed" | "opening" | "open" | "closing"
    setOpen(open: boolean, reason?: string): void
    toggle(): void
    attachTrigger(el): off
    attachContent(el): off
    attachOverlay(el): off
    attachTitle(el): off
    attachDescription(el): off
    attachClose(el): off
    destroy(): void
}
```

## Painted attributes

Same as dialog: `data-open` / `data-status` / `data-placement` on content +
overlay, `aria-modal`, and `role="alertdialog"` (instead of `"dialog"`).

## Wrapper (`<lite-alert-dialog>`)

```html
<lite-alert-dialog>
  <button data-trigger>Delete account</button>
  <div data-overlay></div>
  <div data-content>
    <h2 data-title>Delete account?</h2>
    <p data-description>This cannot be undone.</p>
    <button data-close>Cancel</button>
    <button data-confirm>Delete</button>
  </div>
</lite-alert-dialog>
```

Attributes: `no-escape` (hard confirm), `dismissable` (allow backdrop close),
`placement`, `container`, `transition`. `[data-confirm]` is wired as a
convenience close-button -- attach your destructive handler in your own click
listener.
